Pro
Github618
Getting StartedMotionAI Agents

Concepts

Motion PatternsEasing ReferenceSpring Config

Motion

Easing Reference

Easing defines how a value changes over time. The right curve makes an animation feel natural; the wrong one makes it feel mechanical or slow. Kinetic UI uses two named curves for 95% of transitions.

The constants

Two bezier arrays live in lib/ease.ts. Import them wherever you write animations.

TSlib/ease.ts
// lib/ease.ts
export const EASE_OUT     = [0.16, 1, 0.3, 1]  as const; // fast start, slow finish
export const EASE_IN_OUT  = [0.45, 0, 0.55, 1] as const; // symmetric S-curve
TSyour-component.ts
import { EASE_OUT, EASE_IN_OUT } from "@/lib/ease";

Curve reference

EASE_OUTcubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)

Fast start, slow elegant finish

Use for

Entering elements, tooltips, menus, modals appearing

Avoid

Exiting elements — it reads as the thing reluctant to leave

EASE_IN_OUTcubic-bezier(0.45, 0, 0.55, 1)

Slow in, peak speed midpoint, slow out

Use for

Sliding panels, cross-fades, layout rearrangements

Avoid

Short durations — the slow start feels laggy under ~200ms

linearlinear

Constant velocity throughout

Use for

Infinite spin indicators, progress bars

Avoid

Any state transition — feels mechanical and digital

easeOut (Framer default)cubic-bezier(0.25, 0, 0, 1)

Similar to EASE_OUT but less dramatic

Use for

When you want subtle, understated motion

Avoid

When you need the animation to feel premium

Usage examples

EASE_OUT — entering elements

The default for anything appearing on screen. Feels snappy and confident.

TSXcomponent.tsx
// Recommended for most UI transitions (elements entering the screen)
<motion.div
  initial={{ opacity: 0, y: 12 }}
  animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.22, ease: EASE_OUT }}
/>

EASE_IN_OUT — sliding panels

Symmetrical S-curve. Great for drawers, sheets, and anything that traverses significant distance.

TSXcomponent.tsx
// Good for elements that move across the screen (e.g. dialogs, sheets)
<motion.div
  initial={{ x: "-100%" }}
  animate={{ x: 0 }}
  exit={{ x: "-100%" }}
  transition={{ duration: 0.3, ease: EASE_IN_OUT }}
/>

linear — infinite loops only

Constant velocity. Reserve it for spinners and progress indicators. Never use it for state transitions.

TSXcomponent.tsx
// Use for continuous loops — never for UI state transitions
<motion.div
  animate={{ rotate: 360 }}
  transition={{ duration: 1.5, ease: "linear", repeat: Infinity }}
/>

Duration guide

80–120ms

Micro-interactions: button press, toggle, checkbox

150–200ms

Tooltip appear, dropdown open, inline state change

200–300ms

Modal / dialog enter, page element reveal

300–500ms

Full-page transitions, sheet/drawer slide

500ms+

Decorative hero animations only — never for functional UI
